_metadata.data_type: SEED — compiled from published sources. Replace with live_jobs.json after running scrape_jobs.py --country GHA
sources: Jobberman Ghana — active listings as of April 2025
WEF Future of Jobs 2025 — Sub-Saharan Africa employer skill demand
Ghana Statistical Service Labour Force Report 2023 — employment by sector
World Bank Ghana Economic Update 2024 — formal labour market trends
data_gaps: Informal sector jobs not captured — formal job boards only
Salary data: most Ghanaian postings omit salary; figures derived from GSS LFS 2023 sector medians in GHS converted at 2025 rates
Geographic bias: Accra and Kumasi account for ~70% of formal listings
Vacancy counts are order-of-magnitude estimates from published reports, not precise scrape counts

demand_summary.source: Jobberman Ghana active listings + GSS Labour Force Report 2023
demand_summary.note: Formal sector only. Informal economy vacancies not captured.

sectors_ranked_by_demand.salary_source: GSS LFS 2023 financial sector median (GHS converted)
sectors_ranked_by_demand.salary_caveat: Fintech roles (MTN MoMo, Zeepay, Hubtel) pay premium over traditional banking.
sectors_ranked_by_demand.growth_rationale: Ghana has one of Africa's highest mobile money penetration rates. Fintech sector growing 30%+ YoY.

sectors_ranked_by_demand.salary_caveat: Software engineers and data professionals earn significantly above median.
sectors_ranked_by_demand.growth_rationale: Ghana Digital Economy Policy 2023-2028 targeting 1M digital jobs. Accra emerging as West Africa tech hub.

sectors_ranked_by_demand.salary_caveat: Private sector healthcare pays above Ghana Health Service rates.
sectors_ranked_by_demand.growth_rationale: National Health Insurance Scheme expansion driving demand. WHO Ghana Country Office active hiring.

sectors_ranked_by_demand.salary_source: World Bank Ghana Agriculture 2024 / MOFA salary data
sectors_ranked_by_demand.salary_caveat: Agribusiness roles (Olam, Cargill) pay above subsistence farming wages.
sectors_ranked_by_demand.growth_rationale: Government Planting for Food and Jobs programme. Cocoa sector modernisation ongoing.

sectors_ranked_by_demand.salary_caveat: Most retail roles are informal. Formal mall/supermarket roles pay above median.
sectors_ranked_by_demand.growth_rationale: Accra Mall, West Hills Mall expansion. E-commerce penetration growing from low base.

sectors_ranked_by_demand.salary_caveat: Private international schools pay premium over Ghana Education Service rates.
sectors_ranked_by_demand.growth_rationale: Double-track school system creating additional teacher demand. TVET Ghana expansion.

sectors_ranked_by_demand.salary_caveat: Skilled trades (electricians, plumbers) earn closer to professional salaries.
sectors_ranked_by_demand.growth_rationale: Accra housing deficit 2M+ units. Infrastructure investment programme ongoing.

occupation_demand.label: Accountants and Finance Officers
occupation_demand.notes: High demand across all sectors. ICAG certification strongly preferred.

occupation_demand.label: Software Developers
occupation_demand.notes: Strong demand from fintech and agritech startups.

occupation_demand.label: Nursing and Health Professionals
occupation_demand.notes: Demand concentrated in regions outside Accra (rural posting requirements).

occupation_demand.label: Banking and Financial Clerks
occupation_demand.data_source: GCB, Ecobank, Fidelity Bank Ghana vacancy boards 2025
occupation_demand.notes: Mobile money agent roles increasingly included in this category.

occupation_demand.label: Shopkeepers and Sales Assistants
occupation_demand.notes: Largest volume category. Dominated by informal sector (not captured here).

occupation_demand.label: Teachers (Secondary and Vocational)
occupation_demand.notes: Double-track system created additional demand. STEM teachers especially sought.

occupation_demand.label: Crop Farm Workers and Agricultural Extension Officers
occupation_demand.notes: Planting for Food and Jobs programme driving formal agricultural employment.

occupation_demand.label: Carpenters and Joiners
occupation_demand.notes: Real estate construction boom in Accra suburbs driving demand.

occupation_demand.label: Car, Taxi and Ride-share Drivers
occupation_demand.data_source: Uber Ghana, Bolt Ghana driver recruitment 2025
occupation_demand.notes: Gig economy ride-share (Uber/Bolt) is major informal employer in this category.

occupation_demand.label: Systems Administrators and IT Support
occupation_demand.notes: SME IT support roles concentrated in Accra. Remote work increasing.
